bio.body:
I'm Nick, a software engineer from Bergen, Norway. I graduated with a Bachelor's in Computing (Software Development) from Western Norway University of Applied Sciences (HVL) in 2021, and I'm currently in my second semester of a Master's in Informatics (Visualization) at the University of Bergen (UiB).

I worked as a Full Stack Engineer at InkyPen AS from Sep. 2023 until Aug. 2025 (company-wide layoffs due to economic conditions), where I contributed to B2B and B2C digital distribution platforms for comics and manga. I have experience with AWS, .NET, React/TypeScript, Unity, C#, Java, Python, Haskell, and more. I've also been a Group Leader for INF214 (Concurrent programming) at UiB and a Student Assistant at HVL.

I'm interested in new technologies and solutions, enjoy working in diverse teams, and I'm not afraid to ask questions. I've participated in Bergen Game Jam three times (volunteered twice), three Ludum Dare jams, and I was previously a Volunteer Coordinator & Assistant-Webmaster for Konsoll (Game Development Conference). Some of my game jam projects are on nickmarcha.itch.io. I'm also into homeserver setups, Docker, and self-hosting—this site's backend (visit analytics, admin) runs on my home server behind Cloudflare Tunnel.

Contact: nicolasmmjoes@outlook.com · Bergen, Norway

Languages: Norwegian (native), Spanish (fluent), English (fluent), German (good command)

inkypen.shortDescription: Full Stack Engineer at InkyPen AS (Sep 2023 – Aug 2025)
inkypen.body:
Note: InkyPen filed for bankruptcy in December 2025. The business and its operations have ceased, and servers and publicly available software have been suspended. Steam announcement (archived)

InkyPen AS: Delivering digital U.S. comic books, manga and European comics internationally.
Sep. 2023 – Aug. 2025 (Company-wide layoffs due to economic conditions)
Full Stack Engineer, Bergen
Home Page (archived), Steam Community (archived), Nintendo eShop, B2B Customer Kodansha

Key contributor in a small, international team, responsible for developing and maintaining full-stack B2B and B2C digital distribution platforms. Collaborated closely with the Head Designer and Project Managers (B2B & B2C) to take features from concept to implementation in a fast-paced, agile environment. Experience with AWS cloud infrastructure, .NET backend, React/TypeScript frontend, and integration with external APIs.

B2B solutions: Built physical checkout flow using Google Address Validation & Places Autocomplete APIs; built "On Sale" management system with support for large-scale CSV imports; enhanced proprietary CMS with product reels, rich text editing, and improved submission workflows; automated proprietary SSR cache-clearing workflows using AWS Step Functions, Lambda, S3, CloudFront, CDK, and the Cloudflare API; overhauled internal dashboard listings using TanStack Table; automated digital metadata product import from an external XML-based database warehouse.

B2C platform: Focused on C# ASP.NET backend powering a React web app (mobile-optimized) and a Unity-based Nintendo Switch client. Led development of an Electron wrapper client interfacing via IPC with Steam and Discord; managed Steam beta deployments. Reworked the Auth0 login system: Admin/staff account differentiation, integrated Steam authentication, enforced session limits, and improved account UX.

Internal Admin Dashboard: Built a support system using AWS SES to ingest user emails and web forms; developed a mass scheduled email system with editable templates; integrated AWS SES email event tracking (delivery, open, click) into internal tooling.

DevOps & Release: Owned full production deployments for both B2B and B2C. Managed complex release processes involving Amazon RDS, AWS CDK, CloudFormation, SAM, and EF Core migrations.

kriskogram.shortDescription: Interactive migration visualization with Ego Focus and Temporal Overlay. React, D3.js, U.S. Census data.
kriskogram.body:
Traditional flow maps struggle to convey human migration patterns due to visual clutter and spatial layout constraints. Kriskograms address these limitations through an abstract representation: nodes arranged along a horizontal axis with migration flows as semicircular arcs. This project implements an interactive kriskogram application with two novel interaction features: Ego Focus (k-neighbor filtering centered on a single node for pattern recognition) and Temporal Overlay (multiple time periods rendered simultaneously in static or animated mode). The animated mode segments arcs and maps segment speed to flow weight, enabling direct comparison of flow magnitudes through motion.

Built with React, Vite, D3.js, IndexedDB, and TanStack Router. Uses U.S. Census state-to-state migration data (2005-2019). Done for INF358 Seminar in Visualization at the University of Bergen.

Full paper / about
Live demo

carouselCaptions.0: Demonstrating U.S. state-to-state migration 2005–2008 using arc animation and speed to demonstrate edge width, as well as edge colors to represent years. Try it live

bachelors-project.shortDescription: Ingest video service to work with VizRT application line-up
bachelors-project.body:
This project was done in collaboration with Vizrt Bergen. The goal was to create a service that could in the most user-friendly way possible act as an ingest server and restream video in a format (NDI) which would be compatible with the Vizrt application line-up. Journalists would be able to through their phones/remote access instantiate a stream on Vizrt closed systems and then use any SRT compatible device i.e. smartphone with Larix Broadcaster to low latency stream video. The video stream would then be available within seconds on the Vizrt system and ready to be used in either their live or pre-recorded production pipelines.

The GitHub repository is private but I have a copy of the readme here. Additionally the Postman backend API documentation is available here.

Norwegian Bachelor Slides

carouselCaptions.0: Demo video demonstrating streaming from a 5G device across the city to the Transmit server using SRT (Secure Reliable Transport), which is then forwarded on our transmit server locally through NDI (Network Device Interface). The connection is set up through a simple QR code scan which opens the Larix App with appropriate settings for instant setup.

raffle-dashboard.shortDescription: A Web App for charity raffles
raffle-dashboard.body:
A web app which was used in a charity drive to gather raffle entries and roll them, and subsequently update a Google Spreadsheet Database. The entries were scraped from an ASPX website using node libraries Cheerio and Axios. A backend API was created using Express and NodeJS to handle the scraping and database updating as well as providing a JWT authenticated REST API for the front end to use. The charity drive has raised … USD so far!

Against Malaria Charity
GitHub Repository for web app (includes documentation)

games.shortDescription: Global Game Jams, Ludum Dare and other games I have made
games.body:
I went to a Folk High School called Åsane folkehøgskole and participated in game jams for some time after. I have 6 projects listed on Itch.io where I mainly programmed in the Unity Game Engine, also Unreal. APK for puzzle game made in Unity with a friend.

Highlights: Dainty Deprivation, Hvite Jaktmarker, Dovre Kamikaze

carouselCaptions.0: Dainty Deprivation — Ludum Dare 41. Motorsport meets Fantasy RPG: drive around, collect magical dust, level up. Windows/macOS.
carouselCaptions.1: Hvite Jaktmarker: Stumme Sagn — Story/puzzle game, 2 weeks, limited budget. Windows.
carouselCaptions.2: Dovre Kamikaze — Global Game Jam 2019. VR first-person sleigh racer and snowball fighter. Rubber chicken yetis, enemy brats, snowy ravine. WIP demo.

badlands.shortDescription: Some small projects I did for a GTA V game server called Badlands
badlands.body: I used to play on this RP game server and made some tools for it. I have 3 of them listed and explained in this GitHub Repository. They all involve the Google Spreadsheet API and were hosted on Autocode.
